rationale.R1.2: The artifact makes several mechanistic claims (arbitration drives 70% of gains, terminology agent drives 40%) whose internal logic can be stress-tested; 'what do you think?' on an academic artifact centrally implicates whether the reasoning holds.
rationale.R1.3: BLEU/COMET deltas and ablation percentages are the primary evidence; assessing whether these numbers actually support the broad claims (model-agnostic generalization, closing the gap with dedicated NMT) is load-bearing for any honest opinion.
rationale.R1.4: The paper positions itself against monolithic encoder-decoder systems and NLLB-3.3B; determining whether the multi-agent arbitration framing constitutes genuine novelty versus repackaging is central to a meaningful 'what do you think.'
